msgs.text:
I treated the prior turns as proposals, not as established facts. The first real survivor is Turn 9’s candidate; Turns 1–8 are either directly refuted in the prior file or superseded by later counterexamples. The prompt’s format and constants are as stated in the attached problem, and the latest candidate is the Turn 9 repair using the dynamic tame-inertia exponent 2π, not a fixed integer exponent.

The candidate remains:sigma,tau,x,y nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1Hereπ=π2 ≡40491355905(modM),−π≡45176306175(modM),and the exponent 50932630783 is −2π modulo M, with 2π ≡34735031297(modM). This is still in the prompt’s allowed syntax: σ,τ are the tame generators, τσ=τ2 is implicit, and x,y are pro-2-core generators.The main reason not to declare victory earlier was exactly the Jannsen–Wingberg/Neukirch proof pattern: one must verify the Demuškin structure over every finite tame subextension, not merely the base maximal pro-2 quotient or a few finite quotients. Neukirch’s report says the proof for the known p=2 case checks, for every finite normal tame subextension, that the maximal p-extension group is a Demuškin group with the correct cup product and group action data.Numdam Jarden–Shusterman also emphasize that explicit presentations for maximal pro-ℓ quotients are known via Demuškin–Labute theory, while the Diekert dyadic result does not cover K=Q2.TAU Mathematics What I added this turn is a uniform proof of the missing trivial tame-inertia cup block for Turn 9’s candidate. This closes the degree-2 gap that Turn 9 explicitly left open.LetΔe,f=⟨σˉ,τˉ∣τˉe=1,σˉf=1,σˉτˉσˉ−1=τˉ2⟩,with e odd and e∣2f−1, and put s=πmodf. Write f=2a m with m odd. Then s≡1(mod2a),s≡0(modm).

Turn 9 already gave the nontrivial τ-character block: after the −2π repair, each nontrivial tame-inertia block has cup matrixBα=I+Ts+T−s.

That matrix is nonsingular: if 1+ζs+ζ−s=0 for an f-th root ζ, then ζs has order 3; but s kills the odd part of f and is a unit on the 2-part, so ζs has 2-power order. Thus the nontrivial τ-part is sound.For the missing trivial τ-block, τ acts as 1. In this block the two norm words become, to second Magnus degree,(xτ−2π)π ⇝x,(yτ)π ⇝y,so the candidate reduces to the unramified-type wordrtriv=xσ−πxσπσyσ−1y−1.

Use A=F2 [T]/(Tf−1), where T is the cyclic shift induced byσˉ. With Schreier variables Xa,Ya for a∈Z/fZ, and S=σf, the linear relation is(1+T−s)X+(1+T)Y=0.This relation has rank f−1, leaving f+2 trivial-block cohomology dimensions after adjoining S, as expected. More importantly, after eliminating the linear relations, the ordered degree-2 Magnus coefficient has an X-block given by the circulant multiplier h(T)=1+P(T)+P(T−1),P(T)=1+T1+T−s,with the convention P=0 when f is odd, since then s=0. There is also a hyperbolic term pairing the surviving constant Y-class with S; any X-S cross term can be removed after the X-block is known to be nonsingular.It remains to show h(T) is a unit in A. If f is odd, h=1. If f is even, then s is odd, so P is a polynomial in A. Suppose ζf=1 and ζ=1. From h(ζ)=0, multiplying by 1+ζ gives 0=(1+ζ)h(ζ)=(1+ζ)+(1+ζ−s)+ζ(1+ζs)=ζ−s+ζs+1.

Hence ζ2s+1=1. But 2s+1≡1(modm)and 2s+1 is odd, so gcd(f,2s+1)=1.Thus no nontrivial f-th root ζ is a root of h. At ζ=1, one has h(1)=1. Therefore h is coprime to Tf−1, so h is a unit in A. This proves that the trivial τ-block cup matrix is nonsingular for every f.So, combining this turn with Turn 9’s nontrivial-block computation, the current candidate now passes the entire second-Zassenhaus / mod-2 cup-product nondegeneracy test for every finite tame quotient (e,f). That is a real strengthening: Turn 9 only proved the nontrivial tame-inertia blocks and left the τ=1 block open. The standard rank target is also consistent with local-field expectations: for finite extensions of Qp, Jarden–Shusterman cite rank [K:Qp]+2, and the tame subgroup calculation gives ef+2.TAU Mathematics I am not marking this as a completed presentation. The remaining issue is the dyadic orientation/Bockstein layer. The degree-2 cup product is now uniformly nondegenerate, but for p=2 that is not always enough to identify the exact Labute/Demuškin isomorphism type: the cyclotomic orientation, visible in higher 2-power behavior and in abelianizations of quadratic-index subgroups, still has to be proved uniformly. The prior finite enumerations are good evidence, but not a proof for all tame (e,f).

ESTABLISHED: Turn 9’s candidate survives a new uniform test. I proved the missing trivial tame-inertia cup block: after linear reduction its X-block has unit multiplier h(T)=1+P(T)+P(T−1), and h is coprime to Tf−1 for every f. Together with Turn 9’s nontrivial-block argument, this gives full mod-2 cup-product nondegeneracy for every finite tame quotient.

GAP: The full dyadic cyclotomic orientation/Bockstein invariant is still not proved uniformly. The candidate is now degree-2-Demuškin-correct everywhere, but a final proof must still show the correct q=2 Labute orientation for all finite tame subextensions and their relevant index-2 descendants.

NEXT: Compute the fourth-degree/Bockstein contribution of the same candidate in the reduced coordinates Y=P(T)X+Yˉ, and prove symbolically that every index-2 subgroup has the local-class-field abelianization pattern: correct free rank and only the unique i-extension producing C4, never spurious C8 or torsion-free anomalies.[BREAKTHROUGH]
